meaning_en: Work done or services rendered for free, especially legal or professional services provided without charge for the public good.
meaning_ko: 공익을 위해 무료로 제공되는 전문적인 서비스나 작업, 특히 법률 서비스를 무보수로 제공하는 것을 의미합니다.
example: The lawyer took the case pro bono because she believed strongly in the client's cause and wanted to help without charging a fee.
tip: 'Pro bono' is short for the Latin phrase 'pro bono publico,' meaning 'for the public good.' It is most commonly used in the legal profession but can apply to any professional service offered for free as a charitable act.

meaning_en: A farewell expression wishing someone a safe and pleasant journey
meaning_ko: 여행 후 안전하게 잘 돌아가라는 작별 인사
example: It was so great seeing you this weekend — safe travels on your way home!
tip: 'Safe travels' is commonly used when someone is about to head home or embark on a journey. You can also say 'travel safe' or 'have a safe trip' interchangeably. It's warm and casual, suitable for both friends and colleagues.

meaning_en: To be extremely bored; to feel so uninterested or unstimulated that it feels unbearable
meaning_ko: 극도로 지루함을 느끼다; 너무 따분하고 지루해서 견디기 힘든 상태
example: I nearly died of boredom during that three-hour lecture — the professor just read straight from the textbook.
tip: This is a hyperbolic expression, meaning the speaker is exaggerating for effect. No one literally dies from boredom. You can also say 'bored to death' or 'bored out of my mind' to express the same idea.

meaning_en: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance or important event.
meaning_ko: 특히 공연이나 중요한 행사 전에 누군가에게 행운을 빌어주는 표현.
example: You're going on stage in five minutes — break a leg!
tip: This expression is commonly used in theater. Saying 'good luck' directly was considered bad luck by performers, so 'break a leg' became the alternative way to wish someone well.
